requirements.requirement: NiX will automatically transform these files without users having to make any changes.
The new application will allow them to continue working with the systems they know without having to intervene directly with the new technologies.

The new application will improve daily process monitoring and problem resolution by reducing the probability of manual errors, unifying processes into a single system, and facilitating error identification.
The system validates credentials.
If valid, access to the portal is granted.
The system queries the list of applications to which the user has access.
The user only sees the applications to which they have access.
If the user has access to only one application, they are redirected to its main page.
If the user has access to more than one application, they select the application they want to access and are redirected to its main page.
Restrict visualization and actions (create, modify, delete) on data according to roles assigned to the user.
The system verifies the user's roles and permissions.
Actions and accesses corresponding to roles are allowed or denied.
Guarantee that data of other clients cannot be accessed.
The system verifies the client to which the user is associated.
Only data of the same client is shown.
Convert any mention of the old name to the new name.
The new name is shown wherever mentioned.
The user only sees mentions of the new name both on screens and in data.
Give or remove access to applications to users.
Allow the administrator to navigate the user application management screen.
Select or deselect from the list of available applications the applications to which the user should have access.
The system updates the data of the affected user.
Each language_id of each content table belongs to an existing Language.
Dates must be valid and cannot be later than the present.
For all applicable tables, there cannot be elements of the same type with the same code.
Authorizations and Cluster assigned to a user must be of the same Cluster type as the user.
Authorizations assigned to a user must be from some of the brands that the associated Cluster contains.
New content must maintain visual coherence with the design of the old version of the application.
The system must be easy to use for any user.
The application must be available in several languages.
The user must be able to perform new operations without special training.
Query operations must have a load time of less than 1 second.
Response time must not exceed 1 second in 95% of queries.
The web application must guarantee continuous and stable operation, ensuring its availability at any time.
System availability must be at least 99.9%, even during intensive usage times.
The web application must support a large number of simultaneous users without degrading its performance.
The system must allow creation, query, modification, and deletion of data to authorized users.
The system must have a secure authentication and permission verification mechanism to ensure each user can only access information and functionalities that correspond to them.
The application must manage a strict separation of data for each client.
Leader blocks any attempt of unauthorized connection and applies certain security protocols.
Portal incorporates the binaries needed for the frontend of both the portal itself and the rest of the portal applications.
Communication Log records all communications that occur within the system, both between microservices and with external services.
Scheduler handles the planning and scheduling of processes within the system.
Resources necessary for the application such as network, database, or servers are offered via the Internet.
There is a schema for each microservice and each client has its group of schemas per microservice.
The message exchange protocol used in this case is HTTP and the data format used is JSON.
Angular is used implementing a Single-Page Application (SPA).
Spring Boot is used to expose a REST API.
Angular 11+ is used for the frontend.
Spring Data & JPA 2.2 is used for data persistence.
Spring Boot 2.2.2 is used for backend development.
Java OpenJDK 17 is used as the execution and development environment.
Amazon EKS is used for deployment allowing the use of Kubernetes (K8s).
Each microservice will be deployed in a pod; each client will have a concrete namespace grouping the application's microservices; there will be a common namespace for the Leader microservice.
